mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods of stable inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expansion and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of monetary tightening reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive returns when FEDFUNDS rises by >0.25% in the same quarter, or the signal reverses direction during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index readings above 310.0 indicate entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 310.0, or the threshold effect disappears when wage growth remains below 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and corporate profit growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns when GDP growth falls below 1.0% quarterly, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >40% of quarters, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during periods of financial stress (VIX > 25).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1.5% signals abrupt consumer demand contraction that immediately pressures corporate earnings forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances following a >1.5% retail sales drop, or the signal fails to predict returns when inventory levels are simultaneously rising.
